research.aesthetic: Warm minimalist jewellery photography: products are shot close-up on bare skin, hands, ears, necks and wrists, mixed with clean product-only still lifes on cream, beige and stone-toned backgrounds. Surfaces feel natural and tactile—matte plaster, warm paper, stone, linen and neutral studio backdrops. Colour grade is soft, creamy and desaturated with warm highlights, muted shadows and gold kept rich but not glossy. Styling is sparse: one to three jewellery pieces at a time, occasional shells, stones or sculptural props, with lots of negative space. Lighting is diffused daylight or soft studio light from the side/front, creating gentle shadows and a quiet luxury feel. Crops are tight and editorial—macro ear, hand and neckline details, partial faces, stacked rings and hoops—with a balance of model-worn imagery and isolated product compositions.
